items.text:
A `migrate` module builds a state doc from a Workstream's existing markdown,
reusing the engine's own parsers (objective frontmatter/status, Iteration
frontmatter+title, parse_plan) - no re-parsing logic duplicated. (migrate.py;
added Workstream.iterations(), also DRYs record_snapshot.)

Verdict extraction is STRICT: only lines matching the stamp format
`YYYY-MM-DD | ACCEPTED|REJECTED [| reason]` become verdict entries (lowercased
verdict, reason or null). Prose mentioning ACCEPTED/REJECTED is NOT captured.
Proven against build-lume 003 (reject->accept) and by stamp-line counts:
build-lume 5/5, lifecycle 8/8, state-as-data 4/4 - no drops, no false hits.

Each built doc passes P1/P2 validation (written via Repository.save_state,
which validates) - a malformed migration fails loudly. (It did: caught legacy
`build`/`review` types; see normalization note below.)

`lume migrate` CLI command migrates every workstream under .lume/ and reports
what it wrote; ran it - wrote 3 state.json files (dogfood).

Idempotent: running migrate twice yields byte-identical state.json (verified
via md5sum, relies on P2's deterministic dumps).

Round-trips build-lume (6 iters, closed, 0 plan, 5 verdicts), lifecycle
(8 iters, closed, 5 plan, 8 verdicts), and THIS one (5 iters, active, 6 plan,
4 verdicts). Counts + statuses match the markdown; THIS workstream migrated.

Scope held: markdown prose artifacts left in place; NO existing mutating verb
rewired to read state.json yet (that is P4).

Tests for build_doc + verdict parsing + idempotency + legacy-type mapping;
full suite green (103).
No dead code / unused imports introduced.

handback:
Added lume/migrate.py (build_doc + strict parse_verdicts + migrate_all) and a
`lume migrate` CLI command; added Workstream.iterations() (also DRYs
record_snapshot). Ran the dogfood: `lume migrate` wrote state.json for all three
workstreams, idempotent on re-run (md5-identical). The boundary validator caught
two legacy iteration types (build, review); normalized them to execution/closeout
per documented supersessions (logged in decisions.md - flagged for your review).
Verified migrated counts/statuses against markdown and that verdict stamp counts
match exactly (5/8/4). Markdown left untouched. Full suite 103 OK. Next: P4
rewires open/transition/close to read+write state.json and regenerate views.
self_review:
Adversarial pass against the DoD:
- The validator earned its keep here: the migration FAILED loudly twice on real
  legacy data (`type: build` x10, `type: review` x1) instead of silently writing
  invalid state. That is exactly the "schema-validated at the boundary" promise
  paying off on its first contact with messy reality.
- Resolved the legacy types by faithful normalization (build->execution,
  review->closeout), each grounded in a documented supersession, not a guess -
  logged in decisions.md and surfaced here for operator review. The markdown
  artifacts keep their original frontmatter; only the JSON state is normalized.
  If the operator wants fidelity over a clean vocabulary, this is the reject point.
- Strict verdict parsing verified by counting: real stamp lines (date-anchored)
  exactly equal the verdicts captured (5/8/4) - no prose false-positives, no real
  stamps dropped. The build-lume/003 reject->accept case is pinned by a test.
- Reuse over duplication: build_doc calls the engine's existing parsers; added
  Workstream.iterations() and used it to also DRY record_snapshot's inline
  comprehension. No second parser to drift.
- Scope held: no existing mutating verb reads state.json yet; migrate is additive.
  state-as-data/state.json will go stale as 006+ proceed until P4 wires the verbs -
  expected per the plan's sequencing, not a defect.
- No dead code: every import used (PlanItem/Repository/Workstream/Path in hints).
